design_philosophy.core_loop: Direct wizard control with real-time micromanagement of summoned creature armies; cast spells, summon units aligned to five gods, maneuver tactically, desecrate enemy altar by sacrificing friendly unit at it to win
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Micromanagement-heavy combat requiring constant attention to unit positioning, formation switching, and spell timing while managing limited soul resources and mana regeneration
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command a powerful wizard channeling divine forces through five distinct god-aligned spell schools and creature types, engaging in direct tactical wizard-vs-wizard combat
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately rejects base-building and worker management typical of 2000-era RTS games; instead emphasizes action-heavy micromanagement and direct player agency through third-person wizard control. Core victory condition (altar desecration via sacrifice ritual) is mechanically unique and forces offensive positioning. Rock-paper-scissors creature balancing (melee/ranged/air) enforces strategic army composition.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High attention demands during frenetic real-time combat; success linked to meticulous individual unit control and formation management. Players must master rapid formation switching via keyboard shortcuts or mouse pattern input.

progression.upgrade_system: God-aligned spell and creature trees; players customize attacks by choosing from spells and creatures aligned to five gods (Persephone, Charnel, James, Stratos, Pyro)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Campaign progression through story sequences; single-player campaign follows protagonist Eldred recounting background and world events through voiceovers and cutscenes
replayability_hooks: Five distinct god-aligned spell/creature loadouts encourage different playstyles
Multiplayer mode supports up to four players over network
Emergent tactical depth from rock-paper-scissors unit balancing and formation micro

world_building_techniques: Voiceover narration by protagonist Eldred to blind seer Mithras
Five-god pantheon with distinct thematic identities (regeneration/death/earth/air/fire) reflected in creature and spell design
Humorous content and professional voice acting (Tim Curry among others) reinforces world tone

faction_indicators: Five god factions (Persephone, Charnel, James, Stratos, Pyro) with distinct visual/mechanical identities
Charnel: death, decay, undead creatures, life-draining spells
danger_signals: Aggressive summoning and sacrifice ritual desecration of altars as primary win condition
environmental_storytelling: Mana fountains scattered across world requiring tactical control
Manaliths erected over mana sources for mana generation advantage
Enemy altars as physical objectives requiring offensive positioning
War-torn world setting established in campaign opening
